commit.message:
Fix map tiles rendering incomplete when scrolling into view

Initialize Leaflet only when container enters viewport using
IntersectionObserver, preventing tile layout issues caused by
scroll animations hiding the container at mount time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway): isolate subscription login from real home credentials (#304) (#304)

`runSubscriptionLogin` now creates a temp isolated HOME directory so the
native CLI tools (gemini, claude, codex) cannot find cached credentials
from the real home. This enforces credential isolation — each AgentIM
credential is obtained via a fresh OAuth flow, not copied from the
official CLI's cached credentials.

For Gemini specifically, `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` and a seed `settings.json`
are also configured so the Gemini CLI triggers OAuth instead of loading
cached credentials.

`readSubscriptionAuthData` now accepts an optional `fromHome` parameter
to read auth files from the isolated login directory.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix micro-buy proposals from AI MODIFY decisions; remove delisted CIVI

AI review can MODIFY a proposal to 1 share (e.g. DAWN @ $12.73 = $12.73
total) when it sees unusual factor values. Fixed in two places:

1. unified_analysis.py: filter modified BUY proposals below minimum
   notional (max(5 shares, $250)) before they reach the dashboard, and
   also reject them at execute time with a clear skip message

2. etf_holdings_provider.py: remove CIVI from IWM and VB fallback
   holdings lists (delisted)

Also cleaned CIVI from largeboi/watchlist.jsonl (data file, not committed).

commit.message:
fix(bulk): return FBL not PM from handshake, fix theme persist + log spam

Bulk/LY handshake returned model_id=PM (e.g. 32) instead of FBL (100).
GUI stored this as fbl_code, causing DeviceInfo.use_jpeg to return True
(JPEG) while BulkDevice.send_frame used cmd=3 (RGB565). Device received
JPEG bytes expecting raw pixels → scrambled display (#54).

Also fixes:
- Theme fallback now persists when no prior theme was saved, so
  --last-one autostart restores the theme on next boot
- Handshake guard prevents duplicate concurrent handshakes from poll
- Removed per-frame DEBUG log spam (~30/sec) that rotated out all
  useful INFO messages within seconds of video playback
- Added all v2.1.2 bulk PM values to _BULK_KNOWN_PMS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v1.3.0: coin containers in coin display, purse zone dropdown fix, local price factor

- Coin container items (chests, strongboxes) no longer appear in the item
  list; they are shown next to coin purses in each zone section, displaying
  how many coins they hold vs. their capacity. Chests fill first; purses hold
  the overflow so spending naturally drains purses before chests.
- Empty chests persist as dimmed rows (occupy a slot); purses vanish at 0 coins.
- GM can move chest zone or delete chest directly from the coin display row.
- Fixed purse zone dropdown always showing "Tiny" (zone field was missing from
  coin slot objects built in _prepareContext).
- FlagManager and EncumbranceCalculator now subtract chest capacity before
  computing purse slot count, preventing double-counting.
- Local shops and inns now support a Price Factor (%) field in the note config.
  Default 100 = normal prices; 200 = double; 50 = half. The factor scales all
  displayed prices and the actual coin deduction at purchase time. A gold badge
  appears in the shop/inn header whenever the factor differs from 100.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: 100% vibecoded

commit.message:
Claude/fix auth flow deployment aj fr u (#52)

* fix: repair auth flow, break admin login loop, switch to Neon-direct db push

Issues fixed:
- requireAdmin() redirected ALL non-admin states to /admin/auth causing an
  infinite loop when authenticated users lacked admin privileges. Fixed:
  unauthenticated goes to /admin/auth; authenticated non-admin to /auth/denied.

- getSessionUser() had no error handling around Prisma/auth calls. Any DB or
  network error propagated as uncaught exception (500). Fixed: wrapped in
  try/catch returning status=unauthenticated on unexpected errors.

- /admin/auth showed login form to authenticated non-admin users who could
  never succeed, perpetuating the loop. Fixed: shows sign-out button instead.

- vercel-build.sh was running prisma migrate deploy with complex stale-record
  resolution that failed on inconsistent _prisma_migrations state. Replaced
  with prisma db push --accept-data-loss (no migration table needed).
  Removed Prisma Platform DATA_PRISMA_* URL fallbacks, Neon-only now.

- db-url.ts: removed Prisma Accelerate/Platform URL fallbacks. Resolution
  order is now exclusively Neon vars: DATABASE_URL -> POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL
  -> POSTGRES_URL for pooled; DIRECT_URL -> POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING for
  direct connection.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EvC7SY9T9xd1qGbVDrxgsM

* fix: align neon_auth.user table, fix bootstrap script, add health-check API

neon_auth table name fix:
- All auth-sync queries (page SSR, API route, client component) now
  consistently use neon_auth.user (the actual table name in this Neon project).
  The route.ts and client were incorrectly referencing neon_auth.users_sync.
  Updated GRANT SQL hint in AuthSyncClient to match (neon_auth.user).

bootstrap-admin.js rewrite:
- Removed prisma.adminUser.upsert() — AdminUser model was removed from the
  schema when Better Auth was replaced by Neon Auth. The script now upserts
  into recalc_admin.user (the correct model) with role=ADMIN.
- Removed DATA_PRISMA_* URL fallbacks; uses Neon/Vercel-Neon vars only.

commit.message:
fix: detect crashed/stopped services by querying actual process state (#20)

ServiceStatuses() and ServiceStatus() were reading from an in-memory map
that was only updated on explicit Start/Stop calls. If a service crashed
or was killed externally, the UI would still show it as "Running".

Now queries the runner's Status() method (which checks isProcessAlive)
and reconciles the in-memory map accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: per-request Supabase client and quote camelCase aliases (#325)

* fix: per-request Supabase client and quote camelCase aliases for PostgreSQL

Remove per-isolate caching of SupabaseDatabaseClient to avoid "Cannot
perform I/O on behalf of a different request" errors — Hyperdrive manages
TCP pooling so per-request creation has no performance penalty.

Add quoteCamelCaseAliases to wrap camelCase AS aliases in double quotes
so PostgreSQL preserves casing in result rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace SQL alias quoting with result row key remapping

quoteCamelCaseAliases rewrote SQL to double-quote camelCase aliases,
but left downstream references (ORDER BY, HAVING, CTE columns) unquoted.
PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase, breaking those queries.

Instead of modifying SQL, remap result row keys after PostgreSQL returns
them lowercased (e.g. workid → workId). This leaves SQL untouched so all
references resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: close DB connection per request and harden SQL translation

- Add close() to DatabaseClient interface and SupabaseDatabaseClient;
  call it via try/finally in middleware and scheduled handler to prevent
  connection exhaustion under load.
- Extend translatePlaceholders to skip ? inside double-quoted identifiers
  and line comments, not just single-quoted strings.
- Add stripQuotedContexts helper so buildAliasMap does not match
  AS aliases inside string literals or quoted identifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#150)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,
brightness control, SSH command execution with output polling, URL browsing,

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix security: add auth to flush-queue and auth-check endpoint

- Add password auth to flush-queue GET endpoint (was unauthenticated)
- Add dedicated auth-check endpoint so login doesn't fire a real debug
  command to node1
- Update dashboard to use auth-check for login and pass password to
  flush-queue

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove old static dashboard.html that was overriding Astro page

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being
served by Netlify instead of the Astro-built dashboard at
src/pages/cluster/dashboard.astro. The static file took priority,
so the new control panel with auth was never being served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove stale redirects pointing /cluster/dashboard to deleted HTML file

netlify.toml had explicit redirects from /cluster/dashboard to
/cluster/dashboard.html which no longer exists (deleted in previous
commit). These redirects prevented Astro's dashboard/index.html from
being served. Removing them lets Netlify serve the Astro-built page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix poll script: add PC nodes and proper target group expansion

- Add PC nodes (neo, vikixii, nexus-prime, steamdeck) to cluster-nodes.conf
  with "pc" role, using Tailscale hostnames for SSH resolution
- Add PC_NODES group and resolve_target_nodes() helper for consistent
  target expansion across all commands
- "all" now targets phones+PCs for general commands (start/stop/restart/
  mining/reboot/ssh), phones only for phone-specific commands
- "phones" targets phone nodes only, "pcs" targets PC nodes only
- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/
  screenshot) return "skipped" when targeted at "pcs"
- control-plane nodes now correctly included in PHONE_NODES

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining commands: scope to phones only and capture error output

- mining-start/stop/level now use "phones" scope instead of "all" —
  PCs don't have xmrig so targeting them caused false failures
- mining-start now captures per-node stdout and reports it back via
  the output field, so the dashboard shows WHY nodes failed (e.g.
  "xmrig not installed", service status, etc.)
- Fixed mining-start command to print errors to stdout instead of
  stderr so run_on_node captures them properly

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig systemd service if missing

The phones run systemd but had no xmrig.service file, causing
mining-start to fail with "Unit xmrig.service could not be found".
Now mining-start auto-creates the service file, reloads systemd,
and ensures the xmrig binary symlink exists before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig config.json if missing

xmrig was exiting with status=2 because /etc/xmrig/config.json
didn't exist on the phones. Now mining-start auto-creates both
the config (with pool/wallet/worker settings) and the systemd
service file per-node before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: use printf instead of nested heredocs

The previous approach used nested heredocs in a function inside a
case block, which crashed BusyBox ash on the phones after update.
Simplified to a single-quoted variable with printf for both config
and service file creation. Uses hostname for worker name.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix bad merge: remove stale function code from mining-start

GitHub merge produced a frankenstein with old function-based code
(MSCRIPT_BOT, build_mining_start_cmd) mixed into the printf version.
This caused a syntax error on line 487 crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Move MINING_START_CMD to top of script for BusyBox ash compat

BusyBox ash on the phones fails to parse multi-line single-quoted
strings containing if/else/fi inside case blocks. Moving the command
to the top level as a single-line string fixes the parse error at
line 487 that was crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

commit.message:
Production hardening: logging, caching, resilience, event-driven core

4 parallel agents implemented major infrastructure improvements:

1. Structured Logging (TS + Python):
   - cognition/src/logger.ts (47 LOC): createLogger() with JSONL output
   - research/src/logger.py + memory/src/logger.py (58 LOC each)
   - Integrated into metacontroller, pipeline, memory server
   - Consistent JSON format across both languages

2. LLM Semantic Caching:
   - orchestrator/src/cache.py (213 LOC): File-based cache with
     SHA256 hashing, prompt normalization, TTL, LRU eviction
   - wrap() transparently caches LLM calls in composer
   - Fix circular import (LLMCallFn defined locally)

3. Circuit Breaker + Resilience:
   - orchestrator/src/resilience.py (339 LOC): CircuitBreaker (3-state),
     retry_with_backoff (exponential + jitter), FallbackChain, RateLimiter
   - Composer: retry(2) + circuit breaker on all LLM calls
   - GitHub scanner: rate limiter (30/60 calls/min) + retry(3)

4. Event-driven MetaController:
   - Workspace subscription via RxJS (react to new items immediately)
   - Heartbeat raised from 500ms to 5s (maintenance only)
   - reactTo() for lightweight event-driven processing with 50ms debounce
   - isIdle property skips heavy processing when nothing happening
   - Wall-clock reflection timer (5min) replaces cycle counting
   - ~70-90% CPU reduction when idle

feat: v0.8.0 — classifier QA fix, routing trace, cold-start UX (#39-#46) (#49)

* fix(security): remove shell injection surface and block directory traversal

Closes #43: Remove shell: true from runGit() in git-helpers.ts. Node.js
resolves the git binary via PATH directly without a shell on WSL, Linux,
macOS, and Windows. shell: true is unnecessary and allows shell metacharacters
in args to be interpreted as shell syntax.

Closes #42: Validate module paths in adf create before path.join. Paths
containing ".." or absolute paths are rejected with a clear error. A secondary
resolved-path check confirms the final path stays within the .ai/ directory,
guarding against platform-specific bypass patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(adf): populate command, unknown-op error clarity, CONTEXT scaffold section

charter adf populate [--dry-run] [--force] [--ai-dir <dir>]
  Reads package.json, README.md, and stack detection signals to auto-fill
  ADF files with project-specific content after charter adf init. Populates
  CONTEXT in core/backend/frontend.adf and STATE in state.adf. Idempotent:
  skips files with non-scaffold content unless --force.

patcher: unknown ops now produce a clear error listing valid op names
  instead of the cryptic "handlers[op.op] is not a function" TypeError.

CORE_SCAFFOLD: add a CONTEXT section placeholder so ADD_BULLET
  section:CONTEXT works immediately after adf init without requiring
  ADD_SECTION first.

adf patch help: list all valid ops with concrete usage examples.

bootstrap/init next steps: point to charter adf populate as step 1
  instead of generic "edit core.adf manually" guidance.

harness: extend SDLC corpus and runner with mixed QA/backend signal
  scenarios that exposed the classifier routing issues filed in #44/#45.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: surface competing completions from upstream fork PRs

For each pending upstream PR at in_review or completed status, query the
fork branch's completions table to surface evidence (completed_by and
evidence fields). This lets posters compare submissions before stamping
and makes competing evidence visible to all users.

- Query fork completions table for in_review/completed PRs (graceful degradation on failure)
- Thread CompletedBy/Evidence through PendingWantedState → PendingItem → UpstreamPRJSON
- Rename "Upstream PRs" section to "Competing Submissions" in detail view
- Render evidence as dimmed italic text below each submission entry

first baseline check-in

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add small-circle clipping to terrain shader to fix Mercator rendering (#64)

The terrain shader lacked the small-circle clipping that the raster shader
has. Without it, tiles near the oblique Mercator singularity produced
degenerate geometry covering the screen with dark pixels.

- Compute unit-sphere position from tile bounds + tex_coord in vertex shader
- Add per-fragment small-circle clipping (same as raster_tile_globe.wgsl)
- Widen terrain viewport BGL visibility to VERTEX | FRAGMENT so the
  fragment shader can access clip_sphere uniforms

execution with loudness normalisation) and SHOOTOUT_MASTER (concatenation
of all chain segments into master audio). Extract reconciliation logic
to shared webapp service used by both audio-worker and video-worker.

- Add gts-webapp dependency to audio-worker
- Implement _process_shootout_audio() and _process_shootout_master()
- Extract reconcile_parent_after_audio() to webapp.services
- Add webapp/sources volume mounts and PYTHONPATH to audio-worker container
